text: Correct logical debate (vāda) has truth as its aim, distinguishing it from contentious (jalpa) and sophistical (vitaṇḍā) debate.
label: nyaya

text: From the Nyāya perspective, AI reasoning constitutes anumāna (inference), not pratyakṣa (direct perception).
label: nyaya

text: Organ donation after death is consistent with dharma as an act of dāna (giving) that helps another being continue living.
label: advaita

text: Viśiṣṭādvaita upholds that Brahman is non-dual yet intrinsically differentiated by self and matter.
label: vishishtadvaita

text: Cetanā (consciousness) in Nyāya is an adventitious quality of the ātman, not its essence.
label: nyaya

text: Adhyāsa (superimposition) of the not-self on the Self is the fundamental error Vedānta corrects.
label: advaita

text: Liberation in Nyāya is the permanent cessation of all suffering and the qualities of the ātman.
label: nyaya

text: Arthāpatti (presumption) is used to explain why a fat man must be eating at night despite fasting during the day.
label: mimamsa

text: Prohibitions (niṣedha) carry equal logical force to injunctions, forbidding acts that produce demerit.
label: mimamsa

text: The atom (paramāṇu) is the ultimate indivisible unit of matter; it is eternal and without parts.
label: nyaya

text: Bhāgavata Purāṇa is the supreme authority on the form and attributes of the personal God.
label: dvaita

text: Nitya-karmas like sandhyāvandana must be performed daily without exception, under penalty of sin.
label: mimamsa

text: Pratyabhijñā — recognition of one's true nature — is the essence of Advaita realization.
label: advaita

text: Dāsa-bhāva (servant attitude) toward God is considered the most stable and eternal of the rasas.
label: bhakti

text: Nitya-karma (daily duties) purify the mind and form the foundation of bhakti-yoga.
label: vishishtadvaita

text: Caitanya's acintya-bhedābheda holds that Kṛṣṇa and his śaktis are simultaneously identical and different.
label: bhakti

text: Nārada's Bhakti Sūtras define bhakti as supreme love for God and simultaneous immortality.
label: bhakti

text: Rāsa-līlā reveals the supreme bliss of Kṛṣṇa's divine play with the gopīs in Vṛndāvana.
label: bhakti

text: The jīva is eternally dependent on Viṣṇu and can never attain identity with him.
label: dvaita

text: The four sātvika-āgamas and Brahma Sūtras are the highest pramāṇas after the Vedas in Dvaita.
label: dvaita

text: The individual self and Brahman are numerically identical — ahaṃ brahmāsmi.
label: advaita

text: Karma accumulated from beginningless time keeps jīvas bound in saṃsāra under God's oversight.
label: dvaita

text: Sañcita karma is dissolved at Self-realization, but prārabdha karma must run its course.
label: advaita

text: The state of jīvanmukti allows the liberated sage to continue acting without bondage.
label: advaita

text: The muktas in Vaikuṇṭha experience differentiated bliss graded according to their intrinsic nature.
label: dvaita

text: Upamāna enables knowledge of a new object through its similarity to a familiar one.
label: nyaya

text: Śrāddha (ancestral rites) performed at the correct lunar tithi nourish the departed soul with subtle offerings.
label: mimamsa

text: Māyā has two powers: āvaraṇa-śakti (concealing) and vikṣepa-śakti (projecting).
label: advaita

text: Avatāras descend to restore dharma, destroy evil, and re-establish the path of liberation.
label: vishishtadvaita

text: Yoga Sūtras define yoga as citta-vṛtti-nirodha — the complete stilling of mental modifications.
label: nyaya

text: Līlā (divine play) of Brahman explains why a perfect, self-sufficient reality creates the world.
label: vishishtadvaita

text: Nāma-kīrtana (chanting God's names) is the yuga-dharma for Kali-yuga, accessible to all regardless of varṇa.
label: bhakti

text: Tatvavāda denies any evolution of Brahman into the world; creation is a genuine act of will.
label: dvaita

text: The three realities of Viśiṣṭādvaita are: Īśvara, cit (conscious), and acit (unconscious).
label: vishishtadvaita

text: The world is a real pariṇāma (transformation) of Brahman, not merely apparent change.
label: vishishtadvaita

text: Yajña connects the mortal realm to the divine — the svargakāma (heaven-desiring) must perform soma sacrifices.
label: mimamsa

text: The gross, subtle, and causal bodies are superimposed on the pure witness-consciousness.
label: advaita

text: Mimamsa's hermeneutic (arthavāda) treats laudatory Vedic passages as supplementing, not overriding, injunctions.
label: mimamsa

text: The five sheaths (pañca-kośa) veil the pure self like husks around a grain of rice.
label: advaita

text: The varṇas establish hereditary occupational duties that form the backbone of Dharma in Dharmaśāstra.
label: mimamsa

text: Genetic modification of organisms can be analyzed through the lens of dharma: does it uphold or undermine cosmic order?

text: Mental health issues like depression can be addressed through the citta-śuddhi practices: tapas, svādhyāya, and bhakti.
label: bhakti

text: Śabda-pramāṇa (scriptural testimony) is the only means of knowing Brahman directly.
label: advaita

text: Nirvikalpa samādhi dissolves all mental modifications in the ocean of pure awareness.
label: advaita

text: Īśvara's inner controller (antaryāmin) pervades and governs all souls from within.
label: vishishtadvaita

text: Nyāya distinguishes right knowledge (pramā) from error (bhrama) and doubt (saṃśaya).
label: nyaya

text: Verbal testimony (śabda) is valid only when the speaker has reliable knowledge (āptavākya).
label: nyaya

text: A soldier following orders to kill civilians cannot cite kshatriya-dharma as justification; just war (dharma-yuddha) has strict rules.
label: mimamsa

text: Brahman is qualified (viśiṣṭa) by its inseparable attributes: the individual selves and matter.
label: vishishtadvaita

text: Liberation is the direct recognition that one's true nature is pure consciousness.
label: advaita

text: The mantra 'Kṛṣṇaḥ śaraṇaṃ mama' encapsulates the complete surrender of the devotee to the Lord.
label: bhakti

text: Śuddhādvaita of Vallabhācārya declares the world to be a pure, non-deceptive expression of Kṛṣṇa's joy.
label: bhakti

text: The Veda is eternal, authorless (apauruṣeya), and self-valid — its authority needs no external proof.
label: mimamsa

text: Ānanda-tāratamya — degrees of bliss — distinguishes muktas even in the liberated state.
label: dvaita

text: Turīyātīta is the state transcending even the fourth, realized by the jīvanmukta.
label: advaita

text: God's existence is inferred because the universe, like a pot, requires an intelligent creator.
label: nyaya

text: Vyāpti (invariable concomitance) between hetu and sādhya is the nerve of any valid inference.
label: nyaya

text: Viṣṇu's form is real, eternal, and made of transcendental matter distinct from material guṇas.
label: dvaita

text: In Rāmānuja's philosophy, individual selves and matter are the body of Brahman.

text: Capitalism's accumulation ethos directly conflicts with aparigraha (non-possessiveness) taught across all Hindu schools.
label: advaita

text: Mumukṣutva — intense desire for liberation — is the culminating qualification for Vedāntic study.
label: advaita

text: The gopī-bhāva is the highest intensification of love where the devotee merges in God's enrapturing beauty.

text: The devotee who surrenders completely (ātma-nivedana) to the Lord is freed from all sin and fear.
label: bhakti

text: Śaṅkara refuted the Sāṅkhya view of Prakṛti as independent by showing Brahman as the sole cause.
label: advaita

text: Anumāna proceeds through five members: pratijñā, hetu, udāharaṇa, upanaya, and nigamana.
label: nyaya

text: Śaṅkarācārya taught that jñāna alone removes the ignorance that veils Brahman.
label: advaita

text: Prapatti (surrender) to Viṣṇu is the indispensable means of grace leading to liberation.
label: dvaita

text: Prapatti (self-surrender) to Nārāyaṇa is a direct means of liberation alongside bhakti.
label: vishishtadvaita

text: The relationship of the world to Brahman is that of śarīra (body) to śarīrī (soul).
label: vishishtadvaita

text: Viveka (discrimination) between the real and unreal is the first prerequisite for Vedāntic inquiry.
label: advaita

text: Bhakti performed as perpetual loving remembrance leads to direct vision of the Lord.
label: vishishtadvaita

text: Śrīmad Bhāgavatam is considered the natural commentary on Brahma Sūtras by devotional commentators.
label: bhakti

text: Environmental destruction is adharma because it violates ahiṃsā toward non-human beings and disrupts cosmic order.
label: bhakti

text: Kāmya-karma yields specific sought results but binds the performer through the fruits of desire.
label: mimamsa

text: Sāyujya-mukti — union with God — is the highest liberation granted through the grace of paramabhakti.
label: bhakti

text: Consistent reasoning (manana) purifies the intellect's apperception of scriptural teaching.

text: Liberation (mokṣa) consists in eternal blissful intuition of Brahman in Śrīvaikuṇṭha.

text: Mīmāṃsā philosophy affirms that kāmya-karma yields specific sought results but binds the performer through the fruits of desire.
label: mimamsa

text: In the bhakti tradition, sāyujya-mukti — union with God — is the highest liberation granted through the grace of paramabhakti.
label: bhakti

text: According to Advaita Vedānta, consistent reasoning (manana) purifies the intellect's apperception of scriptural teaching.
label: advaita

text: Mīmāṃsā philosophy affirms that the pañca-mahāyajñas obligate every gṛhastha to daily offerings to Brahman, gods, ancestors, beings, and guests.
label: mimamsa

text: In Rāmānuja's Viśiṣṭādvaita, liberation (mokṣa) consists in eternal blissful intuition of Brahman in Śrīvaikuṇṭha.
label: vishishtadvaita

text: In the bhakti tradition, uttama-bhakti (supreme devotion) is free from any material desire and dedicated solely to pleasing Kṛṣṇa.
label: bhakti
